title: The Walking Grove

features.name: the grove in motion
features.desc-on-touch: eucalyptus trunks that learn to walk in the dusk, roots lifting like feet, the canopy shifting as they move toward some direction only trees can read

features.name: the congregation learning separation
features.desc-on-touch: where the canopy held what the ravine divides, and the canopy learned to walk and leave, and to hold absence as well as it once held communion

features.name: the reading through roots
features.desc-on-touch: paths that show how trees read each other upside down, through the earth; the walking grove teaches by leaving footprints that are also branches

features.name: the destination that moves
features.desc-on-touch: where the grove is walking toward, receding with every step, because trees teach that arriving is a mistake and leaving is the only grammar they know

inscription: the congregation learns to walk and leaves its canopy behind; the trees read each other's departures as a new form of grammar, each footfall a word in a language that means goodbye

file.name: Walking_Grove_Migration_Record_Incomplete.mp3
file.note: a field recording of trees moving in the dusk, their sounds like speech, like footsteps, like a language learning to say goodbye

exits.label: back to the canopy before it learned to walk
toward deeper groves where walking becomes falling
toward the destination that keeps moving
